_doc: Embedded tactical visuals for Edgy Content That Converts. {palette} resolves at runtime.

embedded-1.notes: Part 3 lead visual. The 5-tier risk spectrum chart with risk score, who it attracts, who it repels per tier.
embedded-1.prompt:
Do not render any of these instructions as text in the image. Render only the labeled content described below.

Editorial reference chart showing the 5-tier risk spectrum for polarizing content. Aspect 16:9.

CRITICAL TEXT-RENDERING RULES:
1. The image must contain ONLY the text pieces listed below.
2. No font names, no typography labels, no instructions inside the image.
3. Spell every word and number exactly as written.

COMPOSITION: a four-column table with 5 data rows plus a header row. Each row represents a tier of polarization risk.

TOP CENTER TITLE (large): "The 5-Tier Risk Spectrum"
SUBTITLE: "Risk score. Who it pulls in. Who it pushes out. Pick deliberately."

HEADER ROW (cyan pills, uppercase): "TIER", "RISK SCORE", "WHO IT ATTRACTS", "WHO IT REPELS"

ROW 1 (Tier 1):
Cell 1: "Tier 1: Strategic Personal Vulnerability"
Cell 2: "3 / 10"
Cell 3: "Buyers who distrust polished founders"
Cell 4: "Old-school enterprise buyers"

ROW 2 (Tier 2):
Cell 1: "Tier 2: Questioning Cultural Norms"
Cell 2: "5 / 10"
Cell 3: "Operators waiting for someone to say it"
Cell 4: "People whose business depends on the norm"

ROW 3 (Tier 3):
Cell 1: "Tier 3: Contrarian Industry Declarations"
Cell 2: "7 / 10"
Cell 3: "Decision-makers tired of legacy advice"
Cell 4: "Practitioners and vendors of the called-out tactic"

ROW 4 (Tier 4):
Cell 1: "Tier 4: Philosophical Provocations"
Cell 2: "8 / 10"
Cell 3: "Buyers who buy from a worldview"
Cell 4: "Anyone whose values diverge from yours"

ROW 5 (Tier 5):
Cell 1: "Tier 5: Direct Audience Challenges"
Cell 2: "9 / 10"
Cell 3: "Readers who needed to hear it"
Cell 4: "Anyone who reads themselves into the post and bristles"

TYPOGRAPHY:
- Top title: bold sans-serif, off-white #FAF9FF
- Subtitle: medium sans-serif, slate #1E293B inside off-white pill panel
- Header row pills: condensed mono-spaced, bright cyan #06B6D4, uppercase
- Tier column: bold sans-serif, off-white
- Risk score column: large bold condensed mono-spaced, bright cyan #06B6D4 to bright magenta-pink #DB2777 gradient row by row (Tier 1 cyan-leaning, Tier 5 pink-leaning)
- Other data cells: medium sans-serif, off-white #FAF9FF in alternating slightly darker indigo backgrounds
- Tier 5 row: subtle bright magenta-pink accent border to indicate highest risk

BACKGROUND: deep indigo #1A0B4B to electric purple #7C3AED diagonal gradient.
ROW BACKGROUNDS: alternating intensity rounded rectangles with thin bright cyan outlines.

WHAT TO AVOID:
- No people, no faces, no warning triangle icons, no fire or lightning emoji icons.
- No emoji, no clip art.
- No text beyond what is listed.

Color palette: {palette}.

Output: a single high-quality 2K reference table.

embedded-2.notes: Part 4 visual. The 100-point pre-post scoring rubric worksheet with 6 categories and decision tiers.
embedded-2.prompt:
Do not render any of these instructions as text in the image. Render only the labeled content described below.

Editorial worksheet showing the 100-point pre-post scoring rubric for polarizing content. Aspect 16:9.

CRITICAL TEXT-RENDERING RULES:
1. The image must contain ONLY the text pieces listed below.
2. Spell every word and number exactly as written.
3. No font names or typography labels.

TOP CENTER TITLE (large): "The Pre-Post Scoring Rubric"
SUBTITLE: "Six categories. 100 points total. The rubric tells you ship or shelf."

LEFT BLOCK (occupying about 60% of the frame): a vertical worksheet with 6 numbered scoring rows.

ROW 1: Category name "01 Truthfulness" with score range pill "0 to 20" and short prompt line "Would you defend this in a courtroom with evidence?"
ROW 2: Category name "02 ICP-fit" with score range pill "0 to 20" and short prompt line "Would your three best clients each share this unprompted?"
ROW 3: Category name "03 Recovery viability" with score range pill "0 to 15" and short prompt line "Do you have a clean path to clarify, double-down, or pivot?"
ROW 4: Category name "04 Personal stand" with score range pill "0 to 15" and short prompt line "Would you happily defend this in a public debate?"
ROW 5: Category name "05 Specific value" with score range pill "0 to 15" and short prompt line "Does the reader walk away with a usable framework or action?"
ROW 6: Category name "06 Originality" with score range pill "0 to 15" and short prompt line "Have you seen this position articulated this way before?"

BELOW THE 6 ROWS: a thick horizontal divider line, then a totals bar reading "TOTAL OUT OF 100" with an empty cyan-outlined score box at the right end.

RIGHT BLOCK (occupying about 40% of the frame): three stacked decision cards, top to bottom.

DECISION CARD 1 (top, red-tinted): header "Below 60" in red, body line "DO NOT SHIP" in bold, sub-line "Most likely originality or specific value scored low. Rewrite or shelf."
DECISION CARD 2 (middle, yellow-tinted): header "60 to 79" in amber, body line "SHIP CAUTIOUSLY" in bold, sub-line "Draft three response comments to likely pushback. Schedule when you can monitor first 90 minutes."
DECISION CARD 3 (bottom, cyan-tinted): header "80 and above" in bright cyan, body line "SHIP AND PREP RECOVERY THREAD" in bold, sub-line "Write the follow-up post before publishing the original. The follow-up is what controls the narrative either way."

TYPOGRAPHY:
- Top title: bold sans-serif, off-white #FAF9FF
- Subtitle: medium sans-serif, slate #1E293B inside off-white pill panel
- Category names: bold sans-serif, off-white, with category number in bright cyan condensed mono-spaced
- Score range pills: condensed mono-spaced, bright cyan #06B6D4 on dark indigo pill
- Prompt lines: medium sans-serif, off-white #FAF9FF at 80% opacity
- TOTAL bar: bold sans-serif, bright cyan
- Decision card headers: bold condensed mono-spaced (red #EF4444 / amber #F59E0B / cyan #06B6D4)
- Decision card body lines: bold sans-serif, off-white
- Decision card sub-lines: medium sans-serif, off-white at 80% opacity

BACKGROUND: deep indigo to electric purple diagonal gradient.
ROW BACKGROUNDS: rounded rectangles with thin bright cyan outlines.
DECISION CARDS: rounded rectangles with thin colored outlines matching their tier color.

WHAT TO AVOID:
- No people, no faces, no checkmark or X icons, no calculator icons, no warning triangle icons.
- No emoji, no clip art.
- No text beyond what is listed.

Color palette: {palette}.

Output: a single high-quality 2K reference worksheet.

embedded-3.notes: Part 7 visual. Backlash recovery decision tree across hour 0, hour 1-6, hour 6-24, day 2+.
embedded-3.prompt:
Do not render any of these instructions as text in the image. Render only the labeled content described below.

Editorial decision-tree graphic showing the backlash recovery playbook across four time windows. Aspect 16:9.

CRITICAL TEXT-RENDERING RULES:
1. The image must contain ONLY the text pieces listed below.
2. Spell every word and number exactly as written.
3. No font names or typography labels.

TOP CENTER TITLE (large): "The Backlash Recovery Playbook"
SUBTITLE: "Four time windows. Three decision paths. The next 24 hours decide whether the post becomes a blip or a story."

COMPOSITION: a horizontal four-column timeline reading left to right, with a single connecting line running through it. Each column is a time window.

COLUMN 1 (leftmost): time-window pill at top reads "HOUR 0 to HOUR 1" in cyan. Title below: "The Holding Pattern". Body bullets:
- "Do not delete"
- "Do not engage emotionally"
- "Screenshot the worst comments for pattern recognition"
- "Do not DM clients yet"

COLUMN 2: time-window pill at top reads "HOUR 1 to HOUR 6" in cyan. Title below: "The Decision". Below the title, three branching path cards stacked vertically:
- Path A pill (amber): "CLARIFY" with sub-line "Substance right, framing failed. Add the missing qualifier."
- Path B pill (cyan): "DOUBLE-DOWN" with sub-line "Vocal subset disagrees, quiet majority agrees. Add evidence."
- Path C pill (purple): "PIVOT" with sub-line "Conversation stuck on side issue. Reclaim the original frame."

COLUMN 3: time-window pill at top reads "HOUR 6 to HOUR 24" in cyan. Title below: "The Response Post". Body bullets:
- "Half the length of the original"
- "Confident, not defensive tone"
- "Comment moderation pass on the original"
- "Proactive client DM triage"

COLUMN 4 (rightmost): time-window pill at top reads "DAY 2 PLUS" in cyan. Title below: "Narrative Control". Body bullets:
- "Calmer, more grounded version of original argument"
- "Drop new value: framework or example"
- "Often the highest-converting post in the sequence"
- "Read by the audience that lurked the controversy"

A thin connecting line in bright cyan #06B6D4 runs horizontally across the top of all four columns, with small cyan dots marking each time-window transition.

BOTTOM CALLOUT BAR: "When to delete: almost never. Only for factual errors, doxxing, or genuinely offensive language missed in editing."

TYPOGRAPHY:
- Top title: bold sans-serif, off-white #FAF9FF
- Subtitle: medium sans-serif, slate #1E293B inside off-white pill panel
- Time-window pills: condensed mono-spaced, bright cyan #06B6D4, uppercase
- Column titles: bold sans-serif, off-white
- Path pills (A, B, C): condensed mono-spaced, uppercase, in their respective colors (amber #F59E0B, cyan #06B6D4, purple #A855F7)
- Body bullets: medium sans-serif, off-white #FAF9FF at 85% opacity
- Bottom callout: small italic medium sans-serif, off-white #FAF9FF inside cyan-outlined panel

BACKGROUND: deep indigo to electric purple diagonal gradient.
COLUMN BACKGROUNDS: rounded rectangles with thin bright cyan outlines, slightly darker indigo fills.

WHAT TO AVOID:
- No people, no faces, no clock icons, no fire icons, no warning triangle icons.
- No emoji, no clip art.
- No text beyond what is listed.

Color palette: {palette}.

Output: a single high-quality 2K decision tree.

embedded-4.notes: Part 1 visual. Platform algorithm behavior matrix showing what 4 platforms do with controversial posts.
embedded-4.prompt:
Do not render any of these instructions as text in the image. Render only the labeled content described below.

Editorial reference table showing how four major platforms handle controversial posts in 2026. Aspect 5:4.

CRITICAL TEXT-RENDERING RULES:
1. The image must contain ONLY the text pieces listed below.
2. Spell every word exactly as written.
3. No platform brand logos.

TOP CENTER TITLE (large): "The 2026 Platform Behavior Matrix"
SUBTITLE: "Same post, four platforms, four reach curves. Plan accordingly."

A table with 4 columns and 4 data rows plus a header row.

HEADER ROW (cyan pills, uppercase): "PLATFORM", "WHAT IT DOES WITH CONTROVERSY", "REACH CEILING", "BEST USE"

ROW 1 (Facebook):
Cell 1: "Facebook"
Cell 2: "Quietly downranks in newsfeed surface. Friends-of-friends spread is throttled."
Cell 3: "Capped, lower than 2022 baseline"
Cell 4: "Story-led emotional posts to existing followers"

ROW 2 (Instagram):
Cell 1: "Instagram"
Cell 2: "More permissive. Reels engine favors strong-reaction content because watch time is dominant signal."
Cell 3: "High when carousel or Reel completion is strong"
Cell 4: "Carousel breakdowns and Reels with provocative hooks"

ROW 3 (X):
Cell 1: "X (Twitter)"
Cell 2: "Most permissive. Recommendation system openly amplifies replies-to-quote-ratio. Quote tweets boost reach mechanically."
Cell 3: "Highest spike, lowest durability"
Cell 4: "Sharp threads with quote-tweet bait built in"

ROW 4 (LinkedIn):
Cell 1: "LinkedIn"
Cell 2: "Rewards strong opinion if framed professionally. Punishes tone, not opinion. Sustained reach over days."
Cell 3: "Long-tail discoverability through search"
Cell 4: "Long-form contrarian takes with substance"

TYPOGRAPHY:
- Top title: bold sans-serif, off-white #FAF9FF
- Subtitle: medium sans-serif, slate #1E293B inside off-white pill panel
- Header row pills: condensed mono-spaced, bright cyan #06B6D4, uppercase
- Platform column: bold sans-serif, off-white
- Data cells: medium sans-serif, off-white #FAF9FF at 85% opacity in alternating slightly darker indigo backgrounds
- Reach ceiling column: medium sans-serif, with the ceiling word in bright cyan accent

BACKGROUND: deep indigo to electric purple diagonal gradient.
ROW BACKGROUNDS: alternating intensity rounded rectangles with thin bright cyan outlines.

WHAT TO AVOID:
- No platform brand logos. No Facebook f, no Instagram camera, no X bird or X letter logo, no LinkedIn in-square.
- No people, no faces.
- No emoji, no clip art, no warning triangle icons.
- No text beyond what is listed.

Color palette: {palette}.

Output: a single high-quality 2K reference table.
